reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND in-page in headless Chrome — real coherent multilingual generation, non-degenerate; desktop+mobile layout checked; page-load conformance clean), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome (Transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, fresh profile). Standalone: Xenova/bloomz-560m loaded via {dtype:'q8', model_file_name:'decoder_model_merged'} (the repo ships the LEGACY split-decoder ONNX, so the file must be named explicitly) and generated 'Translate to French: The weather is nice today.' -> 'Aujourd'hui, le temps s'ameliore bien.' — coherent, non-degenerate multilingual output. In-page (CDP driver, fresh profile -> Download ~350 MB -> auto-init -> Generate): the index streamed the same correct French translation token-by-token on WASM (7 tokens), 0 console errors. NOT faked, NOT degenerate.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer callbacks so the UI never blocks. WebGPU is attempted as an optional accelerator with an honest fallback to the verified WASM q8 path.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = a text completion of a PLAIN prompt (BLOOMZ has no chat template). The pipeline returns generated_text; the page slices off the prompt prefix and streams the continuation. Semantic sanity confirmed: correct French translation, and multilingual instruction-following across the example prompts. Small-model caveat (facts can be wrong) is disclosed on the page, not hidden.
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT decoder family (BLOOM/BLOOMZ — ALiBi positions, 250 680-token multilingual vocab) not represented by any other built LLM (SmolLM/Llama/Qwen/Gemma/Phi/etc.).

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/bloomz-560m, linked, plus the original bigscience/bloomz-560m), the task (text-generation), the architecture (BLOOM decoder, ALiBi, 250k vocab, ~560M), the xP3 multitask finetuning, quantisation (q8, legacy onnx/decoder_model_merged_quantized.onnx), runtime/backend (Transformers.js / WebAssembly), size (~350 MB), and license (BigScience BLOOM RAIL v1.0).
rubric.notes: The 'How the API works' section discloses the legacy layout + the required model_file_name:'decoder_model_merged' load option so the demo is reproducible.

rubric.evidence: Every page loads its model through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'Xenova/bloomz-560m', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:350}); a valid current on-device model auto-initialises with no Load click; only Download/Re-download/Update surface otherwise; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/bloomz-560m so the ~350 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee. Verified: fresh profile -> Download -> auto-init ready in the CDP driver.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/generating status is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Generating.../Done status line reports progress and a Stop button interrupts generation. Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Generation failed: ...').
rubric.notes: No WebGPU required — runs on the WASM path everywhere; conformance runs-without-webgpu-fallback holds.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the prompt textarea, multilingual example chips (real buttons), Generate/Stop, max-tokens slider, Sample checkbox + temperature slider (enabled only when sampling), and the 'show next-token probabilities' button. Ladder adds a tool/target-language selector (practical), temperature+max sliders and a run-both button (wild), and a notes textarea + question + retrieve-and-answer (multi-model).
rubric.notes: Ladder: basics (free-form prompt), practical (translate/summarise/classify/answer toolbox), wild (greedy vs sampled side-by-side), multi-model (MiniLM retrieval -> BLOOMZ answer, RAG).

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real Transformers.js text-generation pipeline; the streamed completion, the token/latency readouts, and the 'see inside' top-12 next-token probabilities (one real forward pass -> softmax -> top-k) are all derived from real on-device runs.
rubric.notes: The multi-model RAG answer is grounded in a MiniLM-retrieved note (real cosine-similarity ranking shown), not fabricated.

rubric.evidence: Every button exposes an accessible name (buttons-have-names pass); status uses role=status aria-live (live-status-region pass); the completion region is aria-live=polite so streamed text is announced; sliders/selects/textareas are native labelled controls. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: The top-k probability bars are accompanied by numeric percentages (text), not colour-only.

rubric.evidence: Layout uses fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + logical properties; the prompt/completion boxes are inline-size:100%; the controls row wraps; the wild greedy/sampled columns use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,260px),1fr) so they stack on mobile. Measured 0px horizontal overflow at desktop (1280x800) and narrow mobile (360x740, DPR3) across index + all 4 ladder pages, light+dark.
rubric.notes: Mobile download cost (~350 MB q8; +~25 MB MiniLM on the multi-model page) surfaced on the page. Composer buttons meet ~44px min-block-size.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; tokens stream via TextStreamer callbacks (postMessage per token) so the interface stays responsive during generation. The top-k pass and RAG embedding/ranking also run in the worker.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — generation never blocks the interface; a Stop control interrupts long generations.

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, stream LLM tokens off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy work off the main thread and keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid long blocking tasks on the main thread.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation runs in a dedicated module Web Worker; tokens stream to the page via TextStreamer callbacks (one postMessage per token); the extra forward passes for top-k and RAG embeddings also run off-thread.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js + mm-worker.js off-thread; UI responsive + Stop works during generation; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: schedule/stream incremental token work without janking the main thread
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Schedule tasks with different priorities so critical work runs first and background work is deferred.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — token DOM updates are small, incremental textContent appends (not full re-renders); the heavy compute stays in the worker so the main thread only does lightweight paint work.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: per-token incremental append in the completion region; no long main-thread tasks observed

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and completion area without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — controls flex-wrap; prompt/completion are inline-size:100%; the wild greedy/sampled columns and the practical layout use auto-fit minmax(min(100%,...)).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile+desktop overflow across index + 4 ladder pages, light+dark

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region announcing streamed status and errors
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; make streamed content live; give every control an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; the completion region is aria-live=polite; example chips and buttons carry accessible names.

openQuestions: BLOOMZ-560m is small, so factual questions are unreliable — the RAG multi-model page mitigates this for knowledge tasks; is that framing clear enough to visitors on the basics/practical pages?
The legacy q8 decoder is ~350 MB — a modern unified/quantised BLOOMZ ONNX would shrink the mobile download and let dtype map cleanly without model_file_name; worth watching onnx-community.
Would a larger sibling (bloomz-1b1) via WebGPU be worth a separate additive demo for better instruction quality?

followUpGoals.goal: If a modern unified BLOOMZ ONNX (model.onnx + quantised variants) lands, migrate to the plain dtype path (drop model_file_name) and add a q4 option to shrink the mobile download.
Add a distinct WebGPU bloomz-1b1 demo (honest requiresWebGPU fallback) for stronger multilingual instruction quality, as a NEW slug (additive).

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for bloomz-multilingual (Xenova/bloomz-560m, text-generation, q8, ~350 MB — BigScience's multitask-finetuned BLOOM, a DISTINCT decoder family not represented by any other built LLM). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED in headless Chrome: standalone the model loaded via {dtype:'q8', model_file_name:'decoder_model_merged'} (legacy split-decoder layout) and produced coherent non-degenerate multilingual output ('...Aujourd'hui, le temps s'ameliore bien.'); in-page the index streamed the same correct French translation token-by-token on WASM with 0 console errors. Off-main-thread worker + TextStreamer; real top-12 next-token 'see inside'. Ladder: basics (free-form multilingual prompt), practical (translate/summarise/classify/answer toolbox), wild (greedy vs sampled), multi-model (MiniLM retrieval -> BLOOMZ answer, RAG). Small model -> facts can be wrong, disclosed on the page, never faked. responsive-check 0px overflow desktop+mobile, light+dark. BigScience BLOOM RAIL v1.0 license recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
